Name: spectral_dynamic_inference
Title: Spectral Dynamic Inference: A Training-Free Framework for Latency-Aware Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that leveraging the spectral stability of activation subspaces can enable dynamic, training-free adjustments in inference precision, thereby maintaining robustness under strict latency constraints.
Related Work: Existing works such as Spectral Quantization Routing and Training-Free Geometric Interference Cancellation have focused on static profiling and heuristic methods for optimizing machine learning inference. This proposal distinguishes itself by focusing explicitly on real-time spectral properties of activations to inform dynamic adjustments in precision and routing, circumventing the need for training.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel framework, Spectral Dynamic Inference (SDI), which capitalizes on the spectral stability of activation subspaces to enable training-free, dynamic adjustments in inference precision. As machine learning models become increasingly integrated into latency-sensitive applications, ensuring robust performance while adhering to strict computational budgets is paramount. Our approach utilizes the spectral condition number and decay rates of hidden states as real-time proxies for inference stability. By dynamically routing tokens through varying precision pathways based on the spectral properties of their corresponding activation subspaces, we can enforce strict latency budgets while maintaining high accuracy. Empirical evaluations will be conducted across diverse benchmarks to assess the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier, focusing on latency-critical scenarios. This research aims to bridge the gap between foundational optimization theory and practical, scalable inference solutions, providing a reproducible pathway to enhance the efficiency of machine learning systems.
Experiments: 1. Baseline Evaluation: Compare the performance of SDI against traditional static inference methods on standard benchmarks under strict latency constraints.
2. Spectral Property Analysis: Analyze the spectral condition number and decay rates of hidden states across various model architectures to establish a baseline for dynamic adjustments.
3. Dynamic Routing Policy: Implement a token routing policy based on real-time spectral diagnostics and evaluate its impact on latency and accuracy metrics.
4. Robustness Testing: Assess the framework's robustness under controlled distributional shifts and adversarial attacks, measuring accuracy retention and computational efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the variability of spectral diagnostics across different model architectures and the challenge of ensuring real-time computations under strict latency constraints. Additionally, the dynamic routing mechanism may introduce overhead that could impact performance if not managed effectively.
